title: cydonia silvestris var. maliformis
intro: Cydonia silvestris var. maliformis, also known as the 'Malum silvestre', is an edible fruit-bearing shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its rich content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cydonia silvestris var. maliformis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are commonly used to treat various ailments in traditional folk medicine.
